title: 5H Protocol Graph Model
description: JSON Schema for the 5H Protocol graph: nodes (individuals, organizations, AI proxies, bridges) and edges (handshakes, org-internal links, verified links). Incorporates all cross-model commentary additions as of 2026-04-10.
authors: Gemini/Google (bridge node type, intent_type enum, EdgeRevocation)

nodes.description: All participants in the graph: individuals, organizations, AI proxy nodes, and bridge nodes.

revocation_log.description: Append-only log of EdgeRevocation broadcasts. Required for right-to-be-forgotten compliance in federated deployments.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this schema. Implementations MUST reject graphs with incompatible major versions.

DID.description: Decentralized Identifier. MUST follow the did:5h: method or a compatible DID method.

VerificationLevel.description: 0 = unverified (email/phone self-attest). 1 = social/OAuth (GitHub, LinkedIn, etc.). 2 = government ID or biometric via privacy-preserving ZK proof.

type.description: Node category. 'individual': human user. 'organization': institutional account with admin-designated gateways. 'artificial_interim': AI or automated agent node owned by an individual or org. 'bridge': gateway node connecting 5H graph to an external network (Signal, Mastodon, LinkedIn, etc.).

public_key.description: Public key used for signature verification and encrypted payloads.

key_alg.description: Algorithm identifier. 'ed25519' is the current default. 'ml-dsa-87' is reserved for post-quantum migration (per Muse Spark recommendation). Clients negotiate.

visibility.description: Profile discovery scope. 'public': discoverable by all. 'fof': friends-of-friends only. 'verified': Level-1+ only. 'custom': governed by acl_list.

acl_list.description: Explicit list of DIDs permitted to discover this profile when visibility='custom'. Ignored otherwise.

blocklist.description: DIDs that MUST NOT be routed to or from this node. Checked at every hop. Implementations MUST honor without revealing blocklist membership to the blocked party.

ai_proxy_allowed.description: Whether this node permits AI proxies to act on its behalf as interim nodes. Default: false.

ai_proxy_config.description: Configuration for AI proxy usage. Only meaningful when ai_proxy_allowed=true.

model.description: Model identifier (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4', 'gpt-4o'). Informational; model_version_hash is the binding commitment.

model_version_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the model checkpoint identifier and system prompt template combined. Consent granted to a proxy is version-bound: if this value changes, a new opt-in cycle MUST be initiated. (Claude/Anthropic recommendation.)

instructions.description: System prompt template governing the proxy's behavior. MUST be consistent with model_version_hash.

capabilities.description: Declared capabilities this proxy may exercise.

consent_expiry.description: Optional expiry timestamp for proxy authorization. After this time, the proxy MUST NOT act without renewed explicit opt-in from both requester and target. (Claude/Anthropic recommendation.)

gateway_consent.description: For nodes of type 'organization': whether the designated gateway role is held voluntarily or as a role requirement. 'voluntary' = employee or agent freely opted in. 'role_required' = organizational policy mandates this position. Conforming clients SHOULD surface this distinction to requesters. Omit for non-org nodes. (Claude/Anthropic recommendation.)

interim_node_count_min.description: Minimum number of interim forwarding hops inside the org before reaching the designated contact.

interim_node_count_max.description: Maximum number of interim forwarding hops. Randomization within [min,max] provides spam damping while obscuring internal structure.

bridge_config.description: For type='bridge': declares the external network and metadata-handling policies. Bridge nodes MUST cryptographically attest to this declaration. (Gemini/Google recommendation.)

external_network.description: Identifier of the external network this bridge connects to.

metadata_strip_policy.description: Explicit declaration of which consent metadata fields the bridge strips before handing off to the external protocol.

attestation_signature.description: Bridge operator's Ed25519 signature over a canonical serialization of metadata_strip_policy. Verifiable against the bridge node's public_key.

vouch_budget.description: For Level-1+ nodes: vouch-limited edge creation budget. A Level-0 node may only establish edges if a Level-1+ node spends one vouch credit. Budget restores slowly (recommended: 1 credit per 7 days). (Claude/Anthropic + Muse Spark/Meta recommendation for Sybil resistance.)

strength.description: Relationship strength. Used as a secondary signal in path-scoring; does not affect consent requirements.

mutual_consent.description: MUST be true for all edges. An edge without mutual consent MUST NOT exist in a conforming graph.

vouched_by.description: If this edge was created by a Level-0 node, the DID of the Level-1+ node that spent a vouch credit to authorize it.

crdt_vector_clock.description: Vector clock entry for CRDT-based federated sync, keyed by node DID. Prevents race conditions during concurrent blocklist updates and handshake acceptances. (Gemini/Google recommendation.)

EdgeRevocation.description: Broadcast record emitted when a node removes an edge. Intermediary nodes MUST prune routing caches on receipt. Backed by cryptographic proof of deletion. (Gemini/Google recommendation.)

deletion_proof.description: Cryptographic proof (e.g., SHA-256 tombstone against the original edge hash) that local storage of the edge has been deleted.

broadcast_signature.description: Signature by the revoking node's private key over (revoked_edge_from || revoked_edge_to || revocation_timestamp || deletion_proof).
